subject: Add support for official gamecube-adapter (#48)
author: James McCarthy
provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md: row 77862a67f; class D; bug B1; P1.9 landed status

impact.effect_if_absent: Official Nintendo GameCube Controller Adapter (057e:0337) will not be recognized or functional; controllers connected via this adapter cannot provide input

userspace_coupling.interface: standard Linux input event subsystem (BTN_* and ABS_* codes)

license_provenance: GPL-2.0+; original author François-Xavier Carton (fx.carton91@gmail.com, copyright 2020); based on ToadKing/wii-u-gc-adapter, hid-wiimote-core.c, and hid-steam.c
notes: ORIGINAL MISTER CONTRIBUTION (not upstream). Created for v5.15 kernel by James McCarthy (PR #48); forward-ported to 6.18.38 by Michael C. Ferguson with single change: replaced hid_is_using_ll_driver(hdev, &usb_hid_driver) with hid_is_usb(hdev) because usb_hid_driver became static in v5.2. INCLUDES BUG FIX for use-after-free on unplug (B1 in patch-provenance.md): original 5.15 version cancelled only work_rumble, leaving four work_connect items dangling; with kfree(adpt), those items' handlers would dereference freed memory. Fixed teardown now stops HW transport first, then cancels all work_connect items before unregistering inputs, then cancels work_rumble. Same defect fixed on probe error path (err_close). Verified absent from vanilla 6.18.38 (no hid-gamecube-adapter.c, no USB_DEVICE_ID_NINTENDO_GAMECUBE_ADAPTER). Config enabled in linux.config (CONFIG_HID_GAMECUBE_ADAPTER=y, CONFIG_HID_GAMECUBE_ADAPTER_FF=y). Uses standard input event codes (BTN_START, BTN_TR2, BTN_TR, BTN_TL, BTN_SOUTH/WEST/EAST/NORTH, dpad, ABS_X/Y/RX/RY/Z/RZ) and optional memless force feedback (FF_RUMBLE). No userspace coupling in Main_MiSTer detected.
